rag_rules: Fix AGREE diagnostics by resolving visible identifier references and ensuring type consistency in generated expressions.
Do not introduce package-qualified identifiers or property references not present in the model.
Avoid placing implementation-level bindings in component type scopes; use implementation annexes for such cases.
generic_rules: Generated AGREE contracts must use only visible identifiers and declared helpers from the same annex scope.
Ensure type consistency: numeric variables take numeric expressions, Booleans take Boolean expressions.
Place assumptions and guarantees in component type annexes only when they refer to visible ports or features.
Use AGREE logical operators and syntax patterns such as `if ... then ... else` for branching logic.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts are enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks.
Assumptions use `assume "label": expression;`, guarantees use `guarantee "label": expression;`.
Equations and constants are declared with `eq name : type = expression;` and `const name : type = value;`.
Assignments are used for implementation-level bindings in implementation scopes.
anti_patterns: Do not generate expressions that reference AADL properties like Compute_Execution_Time as AGREE variables.
Do not place implementation-level guarantees or assignments in component type annexes.
Do not introduce package-qualified identifiers or types not visible in the current AADL scope.
Do not assume that AADL property names are automatically available as AGREE variables.
irrelevant_topics: AADL Inspector diagnostics about package or property set visibility
AGREE validator diagnostics (empty in this case)
Model fusion or insertion location decisions
